mnemonics.title: Imaging System Components
mnemonics.content: APSD - Acquisition, Processing, Storage, Display - Remember as 'Always Process Store Display' or 'A Picture Shows Details'

mnemonics.title: RGB Primary Colors
mnemonics.content: R(ed) G(reen) B(lue) - Think 'Red Goes Badly' for additive mixing, or remember monitors emit these three colors to create all others

mnemonics.title: CMYK Subtractive Colors
mnemonics.content: C(yan) M(agenta) Y(ellow) K(ey/Black) - Remember as 'Colors Make Your Kopies' (archaic term for copies)

mnemonics.title: Pixel Bits Calculation
mnemonics.content: For colors: 2^n where n=bits. For grayscale: 2^8 = 256 levels (one byte per pixel). For RGB: 3 bytes (24 bits) per pixel.

mnemonics.title: CCD vs CMOS
mnemonics.content: CCD = Clean, Cheap, Dedicated (higher quality); CMOS = Cheaper, More versatile, On-chip processing (lower power)

acronyms.usage: Type of image sensor known for high image quality and low noise, used in professional cameras and scanners.
Type of image sensor with low power consumption, used in smartphones and budget cameras.
Additive color model used in digital displays and cameras.
Subtractive color model used in color printing.
Image file format using lossy compression, ideal for photographs.
Image format supporting lossless compression and transparency.
High-quality image format used in printing and archiving.
Image format supporting simple animation and transparency.

visualTips.visualization: Think of a pixel as a single tile in a mosaic - each tile has one color, and millions together form the complete image.
Sampling = where you place grid points (determines resolution); Quantization = what color value you assign at each point (determines color depth)
Imagine three flashlights (R, G, B) overlapping on a white screen - overlapping areas create new colors (R+G=Yellow, R+B=Magenta, G+B=Cyan, all three=White)
Think of mixing paint - adding more colors absorbs more light, eventually making the mixture darker (unlike light)
When resolution is too low, diagonal lines appear jagged (staircase effect) - like trying to draw a smooth curve with only corner pieces

analogies.analogy: Like a digital photograph made of millions of tiny colored tiles in a mosaic - up close you see individual tiles, from far away you see the complete picture.
Like the number of dots in a printed photograph - more dots (pixels) means smoother, more detailed image, just like higher DPI in printing.
Like the number of colors in a paint palette - 1-bit is only black and white (two colors), 8-bit is 256 shades (like basic watercolor set), 24-bit is millions (like full professional palette).

CCD is like a dedicated professional photographer who takes time for perfect shots but needs more resources. CMOS is like a snapshot camera - faster, uses less battery, but may have slightly more noise.
JPEG compression is like summarizing a long story - you keep the main points but lose some details. PNG is like making a photocopy - exact duplicate but larger file.
